indication: Coronary artery disease with three-vessel involvement, heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, periodic reassessment of ischemic burden
technique: Rest and pharmacologic stress myocardial perfusion SPECT imaging was performed using Tc-99m sestamibi. Regadenoson was administered for pharmacologic stress. Gated SPECT images were obtained at rest.

protocol.stressResponse: Heart rate increased from 70 to 92 bpm. Blood pressure 146/82 at baseline, 140/74 at peak stress.
protocol.symptoms: No chest pain. Mild dyspnea noted, resolved within 2 minutes.
protocol.ecg: Baseline: Atrial fibrillation with controlled ventricular response. Stress: No diagnostic ST changes. Atrial fibrillation with rate to 92 bpm.

perfusion.stress: Moderate fixed perfusion defect involving the inferior and inferolateral walls (approximately 15% of the myocardium). No new reversible defects compared to prior study.
perfusion.rest: Moderate fixed perfusion defect in the inferior and inferolateral walls, unchanged from stress images. No significant reversibility.

function.wallMotion: Akinesis of the inferior wall. Hypokinesis of the inferolateral wall. Remaining segments with mild global hypokinesis.

impression: Moderate fixed perfusion defect involving the inferior and inferolateral walls (approximately 15% of the myocardium), consistent with prior infarction in the RCA territory. Stable compared to prior MPI.
No significant reversible perfusion defects to suggest inducible ischemia.
Severely reduced left ventricular systolic function (LVEF 33%) with regional wall motion abnormalities, stable.
Previously noted mild reversible lateral wall defect has resolved, possibly reflecting improved collateral flow or optimized medical therapy.
No evidence of significant new ischemia to warrant revascularization.
recommendations: Continue optimal medical therapy for ischemic cardiomyopathy
No nuclear evidence of significant new ischemia at this time
comparison: MPI dated 2021-05-18. Fixed defect is stable. Previously noted mild reversible lateral wall defect has resolved. LVEF stable (32% prior, 33% current).
